text: When you encounter a bug in your code, the first step is to identify exactly where and why the problem occurs. Instead of guessing, use a systematic approach: reproduce the bug consistently, then isolate the faulty section. Debugging tools like breakpoints and step-through execution help you examine your program’s behaviour line by line. Additionally, logging is invaluable—insert print statements or use a logging library to track variable values and program flow. This way, you get a clear record of what happened before the error. By combining these strategies, you make debugging less frustrating and more effective.
